seo_title: Current Time in Abu Dhabi — GST (UTC+4)
seo_description: Current time in Abu Dhabi, UAE. Gulf Standard Time (GST, UTC+4) year-round — no DST. Live clock and time differences for Abu Dhabi.

faq.question: What time is it in Abu Dhabi right now?
faq.answer: Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates uses Gulf Standard Time (GST, UTC+4) year-round. The UAE does not observe Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is Asia/Dubai (shared with Dubai and all of UAE). Abu Dhabi is the capital of the UAE and shares the same time zone as Dubai. The live clock above shows the current Abu Dhabi time.

faq.question: What time zone is Abu Dhabi in?
faq.answer: Abu Dhabi is in the Gulf Standard Time zone (GST, UTC+4) year-round. The IANA identifier is Asia/Dubai. The UAE does not observe Daylight Saving Time, so Abu Dhabi stays at UTC+4 every day of the year. Abu Dhabi shares its time zone with Dubai, Sharjah, and the rest of the UAE, as well as Oman (Muscat).

faq.question: Does Abu Dhabi observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Abu Dhabi and the UAE do not observe Daylight Saving Time. The UAE uses Gulf Standard Time (GST, UTC+4) permanently year-round. The Gulf region, including Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, and Oman, does not observe DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Abu Dhabi and London?
faq.answer: Abu Dhabi (GST, UTC+4) is 4 hours ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During British Summer Time (BST, UTC+1), Abu Dhabi is 3 hours ahead of London. The UAE does not observe DST, so the gap changes when the UK adjusts its clocks.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Abu Dhabi and New York?
faq.answer: Abu Dhabi (GST, UTC+4) is 9 hours ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US DST (EDT, UTC-4), Abu Dhabi is 8 hours ahead of New York. The UAE does not observe DST.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Abu Dhabi?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Abu Dhabi are 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM GST, Monday through Friday. Many government offices follow a 7:30 AM to 3:30 PM schedule. Abu Dhabi is the capital and largest emirate of the UAE, home to major sovereign wealth funds including ADIA (Abu Dhabi Investment Authority) and Mubadala. The emirate is a major oil and gas producer and increasingly a global financial hub.
